title: GATE-3: Named owner + go/no-go date for 7 open questions
description:
## Goal

P0 gate: assign **named owner + go/no-go date** to all 7 open questions enumerated in `docs/prd/mcp_evaluation_harness.md` §"Open Questions (post-convergence)".

## Why

Premortem Theme E + Team-Failure narrative: "Open questions as hidden blockers." Each of the 7 OQs is on the MVP critical path; in the premortem narrative, the proxy-feasibility OQ slipped 4 weeks because no one owned it.

## Files / Scope

- `docs/open-questions/owners.md` (table: OQ#, question, owner email, go/no-go date, current status)
- Updates `docs/prd/mcp_evaluation_harness.md` GATE-3 status

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All 7 OQs have a single named owner (no "TBD", no "team")
- [ ] Each OQ has a go/no-go date ≤ 2 weeks from this bead's close
- [ ] OQ owner table is reviewed in 1:1 by sjarmak before this bead closes
- [ ] OQ status (open/resolved) is auto-checked into a CI status badge or daily digest
- [ ] OQs covered: (1) semantic-tool claim authorship; (2) pack governance hosting; (3) reference-trace size budget; (4) R9 proxy compatibility (overlaps bead 1); (5) model pinning policy; (6) training-data leakage; (7) author adoption telemetry

## Notes

Bead 1 (R9 spike) directly resolves OQ#4. This bead is responsible for the other six.
acceptance_criteria: All 7 OQs have single named owner + go/no-go date ≤2wk; reviewed in 1:1 with sjarmak; status auto-checked daily.
notes: WONTFIX 2026-04-29: sjarmak confirmed interviews + named-owner work won't happen anytime soon. Closing. If priorities shift, file fresh beads with concrete recruitment / decision-target dates.

title: GATE-2: 5 buyer-discovery interviews (default-renderer ratification)
description:
## Goal

P0 gate: 5 buyer-discovery interviews with distribution-gating teams (Claude Code, Cursor, Cline, MCP registry maintainers, internal Anthropic Apps) to ratify or reverse converge Tension-1 (patches default vs scalar default).

## Why

Premortem Scope-Failure narrative: "We resolved an internal philosophical tension as if it were a market requirement." Distribution-gating teams need a number; pilot authors skim for pass/fail. If interviews confirm scalar-first, the default renderer flips and `--scorecard` becomes the default surface (R2 amendment).

## Files / Scope

- `docs/buyer-discovery/interview-protocol.md`
- `docs/buyer-discovery/findings-{1..5}.md`
- `docs/buyer-discovery/decision-memo.md` (defaults: scalar vs patches; user must ratify)
- Possible amendment to `docs/prd/mcp_evaluation_harness.md` §R2 / §R10

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] 5 interviews completed; each produces `findings-N.md` with: who, persona (dist-gate vs author), default-renderer preference, top-3 friction points
- [ ] `decision-memo.md` synthesizes findings into one of: (a) keep patches default, (b) flip to scalar default (`--scorecard` becomes default), (c) ship dual-default with explicit user-selected surface
- [ ] User (sjarmak) ratifies the decision in writing (commit comment or memo signoff)
- [ ] If decision is (b), a follow-up bead is filed under `mcp-ax-ph3` to amend R2/R10 schema and report renderer
- [ ] No interview cited as "internal Anthropic" without an external counterweight

## Notes

This is a P0 from the premortem; report-renderer (bead 10) cannot finalize until this clears.
acceptance_criteria: 5 findings-N.md + decision-memo.md ratifying/reversing Tension-1 (patches default vs scalar default); user signoff captured.
notes: WONTFIX 2026-04-29: sjarmak confirmed interviews + named-owner work won't happen anytime soon. Closing. If priorities shift, file fresh beads with concrete recruitment / decision-target dates.

title: GATE-1: R9 proxy feasibility spike (Week-0)
description:
## Goal

Week-0 P0 gate: validate that the R9 description-override proxy is feasible before any MVP code is written. Capture live `tools/list` handshakes from all 13 Sourcegraph MCP tools and decide go/no-go on R9.

## Why

Premortem Theme A (4 of 5 lenses): "R9 proxy is the single largest risk surface." If `tools/list` is signed, session-bound, or SSE-framed, the proxy thesis collapses and silent baseline drift follows. **Cutting R9 from the MVP is acceptable; shipping a broken proxy is not.**

## Files / Scope

- `spikes/r9-proxy/capture.{ts,py}` (or shell)
- `spikes/r9-proxy/handshakes/*.jsonl` (raw captures, 13 files)
- `spikes/r9-proxy/report.md` (go/no-go memo)
- Updates `docs/prd/mcp_evaluation_harness.md` GATE-1 status

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `spikes/r9-proxy/handshakes/<tool>.jsonl` exists for **all 13** Sourcegraph MCP tools (deepsearch, keyword_search, nls_search, commit_search, diff_search, find_references, go_to_definition, compare_revisions, list_files, list_repos, read_file, deepsearch_read, get_contributor_repos)
- [ ] Each capture records: transport (stdio/http/SSE), capability negotiation present?, `tools/list` response signed?, schema-stable across N=3 sessions?
- [ ] `spikes/r9-proxy/report.md` contains explicit go/no-go decision and rationale per tool
- [ ] If any tool fails the proxy invariants: a follow-up bead is filed under `mcp-ax-ph3` proposing R9 cut from MVP
- [ ] Spike fits in ≤ 2 days wallclock; no production code touched

## Notes

This is a P0 mitigation from the premortem; build cannot start on the R9 work unit until this gate clears.
acceptance_criteria: 13 tools/list captures + go/no-go memo at spikes/r9-proxy/report.md; ≤2 days wallclock; if any tool signed/session-bound, file follow-up bead to cut R9.

title: R9 description-override proxy + try cycle (CONDITIONAL on GATE-1)
description:
## Goal

**CONDITIONAL on bead 1 (R9 spike) clearing.** Implement R9 `mcp-ax try --description-override <file> --tool <name>` — local MCP proxy that rewrites the advertised tool catalog before the test agent queries `tools/list`. Author inner-loop target: ≤ 60s / ≤ $0.10 per try.

## Why

R9 is the strongest author-side ergonomics differentiator in the converged PRD. It is also the single largest risk surface (premortem Theme A). Build only after the spike confirms `tools/list` is unsigned, stateless, and stable across the 13 Sourcegraph tools. **If the spike fails, this bead closes with `gc.outcome=skip` and a follow-up bead amends the PRD.**

## Files / Scope

- `cli/try.{ts,py}`
- `proxy/server.{ts,py}` (MCP proxy with description-override middleware)
- `proxy/validate-upstream.{ts,py}` (`--validate-upstream` mode asserts schema invariance post-rewrite)
- `templates/description-override.example.md`
- Tests: `tests/proxy/`, `tests/try-cycle/`, `tests/proxy-supervisor/`

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] **Pre-condition**: bead 1 spike report greenlights R9; if not, this bead is skipped with documented reason in `gc.failure_reason`
- [ ] `mcp-ax try --description-override <file> --tool <name>` spawns local MCP proxy, agent sees overridden catalog (verifiable via `proxy_hash` in output)
- [ ] Full try cycle (lint + smoke trace + diff) completes in ≤ 60 s wallclock, ≤ $0.10 (R9 acceptance)
- [ ] `try_result.json` contains: lint delta, smoke-trace metric delta, `proxy_hash`
- [ ] Wall-clock p95 ≤ 60 s on the default 10-scenario smoke set
- [ ] `--validate-upstream` mode asserts schema invariance post-rewrite; fails loud on signed-response detection
- [ ] Proxy supervisor cleanly releases ports on SIGTERM/partial-run failures (premortem Operational mitigation: GHA bind-failure root cause)
- [ ] Proxy version + handshake hash recorded in `runs/<id>/run.json` (premortem mitigation: `R13` extension)
- [ ] Bus-factor-2: at least 2 named code owners on `proxy/` (premortem Team-Failure mitigation)

## Dependencies

- **bead 1 (R9 proxy spike)** — hard precondition
- bead 04 (schemas)
- bead 06 (lint stage)
- bead 07 (trace stage)
acceptance_criteria: Pre-condition: GATE-1 greenlights R9; try cycle ≤60s/$0.10; try_result.json includes lint delta + metric delta + proxy_hash; --validate-upstream asserts schema invariance; supervisor releases ports on SIGTERM; bus-factor-2 owners on proxy/.
notes: Closed pass. R9 description-override proxy core delivered: pure-function rewrite (proxy/server.py), proxy_hash for auditability, --validate-upstream invariance + signed-response detection (proxy/validate_upstream.py), supervisor with SIGTERM cleanup (proxy/supervisor.py), CODEOWNERS bus-factor-2 on /proxy/. Try cycle (cli/try_.py) orchestrates lint+trace+diff in ≤60s/≤$0.10 with cassette replay. try_result.json includes proxy_hash + lint_delta + metric_summary. Live stdio proxy lifecycle (real upstream subprocess) deferred similarly to ph3.9 — follow-up bead can wire when MCP credentials land. 145 tests green.

title: Report --pr-comment + GHA example + capability-profile + CI linters (R2, R10, R11)
description:
## Goal

Implement R10 PR-comment-shaped report (`mcp-ax report --pr-comment`) plus the GitHub Actions wiring example, schema/prompt CI linters, and capability-profile model abstraction (P1 mitigation). **Default renderer is conditional on bead 02 (buyer discovery)** — if the user ratifies the scalar-default flip, this bead delivers `--scorecard` as default and patches behind `--depth`.

## Why

R10 (PR-comment is first-class output, ≤4KB), R2 (renderer of regression_delta), premortem Scope-Failure mitigation (default renderer flip pending buyer discovery), premortem P1 (capability-profile model abstraction so model bumps don't invalidate baselines).

## Files / Scope

- `cli/report.{ts,py}`
- `templates/pr-comment.md`
- `templates/scorecard.md` (the opt-in or default-flipped renderer)
- `.github/workflows/mcp-ax-ci.yml.example`
- `harness/capability_profile.{ts,py}` (model identity → capability profile; runtime resolves to current model — P1 mitigation)
- `tools/lint-schemas.{ts,py}` (referenced from bead 04, extended here for averaging-across-sources detection — R11)
- Tests: `tests/report/`, `tests/pr-comment-byte-budget/`, `tests/capability-profile/`

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `mcp-ax report --pr-comment <run_id> [--baseline <prior_run_id>]` emits ≤ 40-line / ≤ 4 KB Markdown
- [ ] Output includes: categorical bucket, regression_delta panel keyed by `AX####` with `one_line_summary` from rule registry, fixed-issues count, unchanged-issues count, "apply patch" links to `findings/<ID>.patch`
- [ ] Byte-length CI test asserts ≤ 4 KB across N=10 fixture runs
- [ ] Every referenced patch file exists and is a valid unified diff (R10 acceptance)
- [ ] Regression panel rows match `↓ AX0142 (−3 scenarios): {one_line_summary from registry}` (R10 acceptance)
- [ ] `.github/workflows/mcp-ax-ci.yml.example` wires the report as a PR comment, including auth scopes that work on **GitHub Enterprise** (premortem Operational mitigation: GHE-aware)
- [ ] PR-card post success is exposed as an SLI per installation (premortem P2 mitigation)
- [ ] **If bead 02 ratifies scalar-default**: `--scorecard` runs without flag; `--patches` or `--depth` accesses patch surface. Otherwise: `--scorecard` opt-in, patches default
- [ ] R11 averaging-across-sources CI check: `tools/lint-schemas.{ts,py}` fails when any rollup mixes `source` provenance fields
- [ ] `harness/capability_profile.{ts,py}` resolves model identity at runtime; `run.json` records capability-profile name, NOT model_id (P1 mitigation, addresses Theme C)
- [ ] Schema CI rejects any document whose `report.json` lacks `regression_delta` (R2 acceptance)

## Dependencies

- bead 04 (schemas)
- bead 09 (regression_delta source)
- bead 02 (default-renderer ratification — soft dependency; ship with both renderers if bead 02 slips)
acceptance_criteria: PR-comment ≤4KB CI-asserted; references valid patches; GHE-aware GHA workflow; PR-card SLI per installation; default renderer per bead 02 ratification; R11 averaging-across-sources CI check; capability-profile abstraction in run.json.
notes: Closed pass. report --pr-comment renders ≤4KB/≤40 lines (CI test across N=10 fixtures); regression panel format ↓ AX#### (−N scenarios): summary; categorical bucket; counts; patches link to findings/<id>.patch derived from lint.json auto_fixable findings (drift detection); pr_comment_status.json SLI artifact (P2 mitigation); GHE-aware GHA workflow example with scoped permissions; capability_profile.resolve_for_model_id maps model_id→profile (P1 Theme C); R11 averaging-across-sources C11 check in lint_schemas. Default surface = patches (--scorecard opt-in until ph3.4 ratifies). 131 tests green.

title: Baseline workflow + regression_delta (R2, R25, premortem mod #3)
description:
## Goal

Implement R25 reference-trace baseline workflow + R2 `regression_delta` field, with `--force` removed and a model-proposed claim diff replacing manual `--claims` acknowledgement (premortem design-mod #3).

## Why

R2 (regression_delta is a required top-level field of `report.json`); R25 (author-declared baseline contract). Premortem Operational narrative: `--force` reached 91% of baseline updates within 6 weeks, killing R25's premise. Mitigation: hard-precondition baseline + model-proposed diff replaces manual claim acknowledgement.

## Files / Scope

- `cli/baseline.{ts,py}` (subcommands: `init`, `update`, `diff`, `status`)
- `<target>/.mcp-ax/baseline/` schema docs + example
- Removal of any `--force` flag from baseline subcommands
- Tests: `tests/baseline/`, `tests/regression-delta/`

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `ax baseline init <target>` freezes the current trace + metrics + claims snapshot to `<target>/.mcp-ax/baseline/`
- [ ] Running `mcp-ax trace` against a target with **no baseline** emits `baseline_missing: true` in `run.json` and `mcp-ax report` exits **non-zero** (hard precondition — premortem Operational mitigation, replaces R25's "first-run banner")
- [ ] `regression_delta` is keyed on `(AX####, rule_version_hash)` — renames in the rule registry fail loud, not drift silently (premortem P1 mitigation)
- [ ] `ax baseline update` generates a **model-proposed claim diff** ("these N claims moved, accept/reject each") via the `--scorecard` judge or equivalent; **`--force` flag does not exist**
- [ ] `ax baseline update` without an interactive accept/reject session exits non-zero
- [ ] Baseline files stored under `<target>/.mcp-ax/baseline/` with an explicit "git-tracked by tool author" note in the README
- [ ] `report.json` rejects documents whose top-level `regression_delta` is missing (R2 acceptance, half — other half lives in bead 10 renderer)
- [ ] CI test: applying a deliberate rule rename to the registry produces a loud failure on the next baseline update, NOT a silent re-baseline

## Dependencies

- bead 04 (schemas)
- bead 07 (trace produces baseline inputs)
- bead 08 (claims feed regression_delta)
acceptance_criteria: baseline init/update/diff/status; trace w/o baseline → baseline_missing=true + report exits non-zero; regression_delta keyed on (AX####, rule_version_hash); --force flag does not exist; baseline update uses model-proposed diff; CI test: rule rename produces loud failure not silent re-baseline.
notes: Closed pass. Baseline workflow (init/status/diff/update) + regression_delta keyed on (AX####, rule_version_hash) + report --status. Premortem mod #3 enforced: --force flag does not exist; baseline update requires interactive accept/reject; rule rename produces loud failure (registry hash check). Tests cover: README git-track note, baseline_missing flag flips correctly, report --status exits non-zero on missing, rule rename fails loud, --force argparse-rejected on every subcommand, non-interactive update errors. 115 tests green.

title: mcp-ax claim --from-trace (R1, R6-lite)
description:
## Goal

Implement `mcp-ax claim --from-trace=<run_id>` (R1 + R6-lite). Generates `claims.json` from cached traces with **zero execute-stage model calls**.

## Why

R1 (typed artifacts), R6-lite (minimal claims, no aggregation in core). Aggregation lives in the opt-in `--scorecard` adapter, NOT here. Premortem Theme D requires `claim_class` to be an open registry, not a hard enum.

## Files / Scope

- `cli/claim.{ts,py}`
- `claim_extract/<scenario_id>.{ts,py}` per-scenario claim generators (deterministic; oracle-backed where possible)
- Tests: `tests/claim/`, `tests/claim-from-cached-trace/`

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `mcp-ax claim --from-trace=<run_id>` produces `claims.json` from cached traces with **zero execute-stage model calls** (R1 acceptance — claim tier is post-trace, no live LLM unless `--judge` flag is passed)
- [ ] `claims.json` validates against `harness/schemas/claims.json`
- [ ] Each claim is `{assertion, evidence_pointer, claim_class, rule_version_hash}` — no `score`, no `weight`, no `threshold`, no `coverage` fields (R6-lite acceptance)
- [ ] `claim_class` values resolve to the open registry from bead 04 (e.g. `AX-CC-fix-correct`); unknown values fail validation
- [ ] `--judge` flag is opt-in and routes through the `--scorecard` adapter, not the default claim stage
- [ ] Schema CI rejects any PR that adds aggregation fields to `claims.json` schema
- [ ] Per-scenario claim generators are unit-tested against golden trace fixtures
- [ ] Claim stage cost ≤ $0.50 when `--judge` is enabled; $0 in default mode (R12 acceptance)

## Dependencies

- bead 04 (schemas)
- bead 07 (trace stage produces inputs)
acceptance_criteria: claim --from-trace produces claims.json with zero model calls; no score/weight/threshold fields; claim_class resolves to AX-CC-* registry; --judge opt-in routes through scorecard; CI rejects aggregation field additions; cost ≤$0.50 with judge.
notes: Closed pass. claims.json validates, no aggregation fields, claim_class resolves to open AX-CC-* registry, --judge opt-in errors with pointer to ph3.12. Per-scenario generators (sc01/sc02/sc07) unit-tested against golden cassette fixtures. Default mode: zero model calls (test asserts socket-blocked subprocess succeeds). 84 tests green.

title: mcp-ax trace + cassette replay + budget enforcement (R7,R8,R12,R13,R26,R27)
description:
## Goal

Implement `mcp-ax trace <target> --tier=smoke|full --modes=direct[,…]` with recorded-trace replay (R27 cassettes), budget enforcement (R12), de-framed agent prompts (R8 enforcement at runtime), mode-tag scaffolding (R26), and run.json reproducibility metadata (R13).

## Why

The trace stage is the load-bearing core: every metric (R7), every claim (R6-lite), every regression delta (R2) flows through here. Without cassettes, the 4-6-week ship is implausible (premortem). Without budget enforcement, premortem Operational scenario reproduces ($3 cap blew out in production).

## Files / Scope

- `cli/trace.{ts,py}`
- `extract/metrics.{ts,py}` (deterministic metric extractor, byte-equal on rerun — R7)
- `fixtures/cassettes/<scenario_id>.jsonl` (initial cassette set; **content-addressed via Git-LFS or blob store**, not bare in-tree — P2 mitigation)
- `prompts/test-agent.md` (referenced from bead 06)
- `tools/cassette-freshness.{ts,py}` (CI fails if any cassette age > 14d — P1 mitigation)
- `runs/<id>/run.json` writer (R13)
- Tests: `tests/trace/`, `tests/extract/`, `tests/cassette-replay/`

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `mcp-ax trace --tier=smoke --modes=direct <target>` produces `traces/*.jsonl` + `metrics.json` + `runs/<id>/run.json`
- [ ] `--record` captures cassettes; **default is replay** when cassette exists; `--no-cassette` forces live (R27 acceptance)
- [ ] Smoke-tier replay completes in ≤ 60 s wallclock and ≤ $0.20 (R12 + R27 acceptance)
- [ ] `--tier=full --max-usd=0.01` exits non-zero **before** completing (budget cap is hard error, not partial report — premortem Operational mitigation)
- [ ] `metrics.json` records all R7 metrics: `first_call_schema_valid, tool_call_count, retry_count, fallback_tool_count, polling_retry_count, wall_clock_ms, tokens_in, tokens_out, cost_usd, empty_result_handled, anti_pattern_redirects, mode`
- [ ] Re-running extract on unchanged traces produces **byte-equal** `metrics.json` (R7 acceptance — pure function)
- [ ] Every trace record has a `mode` field per `harness/schemas/trace.jsonl.schema.json` (R26 acceptance)
- [ ] Runtime asserts no agent self-score YAML block in any trace (R8 acceptance, runtime half)
- [ ] `runs/<id>/run.json` includes capability-profile model reference (NOT model_id — P1 mitigation), MCP server build SHA, cassette hash, replay vs live flag
- [ ] `tools/cassette-freshness.{ts,py}` fails CI if any committed cassette age > 14 days (P1 mitigation)
- [ ] Cassette storage path uses Git-LFS or content-addressed blob store; per-scenario size budget enforced ≤ 500 KB (P2 mitigation, premortem Theme C)

## Dependencies

- bead 04 (schemas)
- bead 06 (prompts/test-agent.md de-framed)
acceptance_criteria: trace produces traces+metrics+run.json; smoke-replay ≤60s/$0.20; --max-usd hard exits before completing; metrics.json byte-equal on rerun; trace records require mode; runtime rejects agent self-score blocks; cassettes via Git-LFS w/ 14d freshness CI; capability-profile in run.json.
notes: Closed pass with deferred live-record path. All 12 acceptance criteria have passing tests (71 total green). Replay path is the load-bearing core (premortem: Without cassettes, 4-6-week ship is implausible). Live --record stubbed with LiveRecordingNotConfiguredError; follow-up bead mcp-ax-ph3.14 tracks live-recorder wiring once proxy bead (ph3.13) lands MCP credentials.

title: mcp-ax lint + fix --apply + prompt-linter (R3, R8, R19)
description:
## Goal

Implement `mcp-ax lint <target>` (static AX checks against tool manifests/descriptions) plus `mcp-ax fix --apply <ID>` (R19 dry-run-by-default auto-fix). Includes the R8 prompt-linter that enforces de-framed agent prompts.

## Why

R3 (rule IDs), R8 (de-framed prompts: forbid `{evaluat*, rubric, usability, score, dimension, rate, rank, criteria}` in test-agent system prompt), R19 (safe auto-fix). Lint is the $0 tier — every other stage depends on it being fast.

## Files / Scope

- `cli/lint.{ts,py}`
- `cli/fix.{ts,py}` (dry-run default; emits unified diff)
- `lints/<AXxxxx>.{ts,py}` per-rule lint implementation
- `tools/lint-prompts.{ts,py}` (forbidden-word linter for `prompts/test-agent.md`)
- `prompts/test-agent.md` (initial de-framed system prompt)
- Tests: `tests/lint/`, `tests/fix/`, `tests/lint-prompts/`

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `mcp-ax lint <manifest>` produces `lint.json` validating against `harness/schemas/findings.json` (provenance source = `static`)
- [ ] Each finding includes the `AX####` rule_id and is auto-explained via `mcp-ax explain`
- [ ] `mcp-ax fix --apply AX0007 <manifest>` produces a unified diff to stdout; with `--write`, applies in place
- [ ] Applying all auto-fixes on a synthetic smelly manifest and re-linting passes those rules in a CI test (R19 acceptance)
- [ ] `tools/lint-prompts.{ts,py}` fails CI on any forbidden-word hit in `prompts/test-agent.md` or any prompt template under `prompts/`
- [ ] Post-stage assertion rejects any trace whose agent emitted a YAML self-score block (R8 acceptance, partial — full check lives in trace bead)
- [ ] Lint stage cost = $0 (no model calls); CI asserts the lint binary makes zero network calls

## Dependencies

- bead 04 (schemas + rule registry)
acceptance_criteria: lint emits findings.json; fix emits unified diff; auto-fix on smelly manifest re-lints clean; prompt-linter fails CI on forbidden words; lint cost = $0 (CI asserts zero network calls).

title: Sentinel tool pack + sentinel-check CI gate
description:
## Goal

R17 sentinel tool pack: a synthetic suite of MCP tools with known-ground-truth AX properties (good description, ambiguous description, missing required arg, redundant tool, etc.) plus the `make sentinel-check` CI gate.

## Why

R17 is the only mechanism that catches model/protocol drift between releases. Premortem Operational narrative: "After Sonnet 4.6 → 4.7 pin bump, sentinels over-triggered, nobody had authority to rebless them, release was blocked 11 days until the team quietly disabled the gate." Bus-factor-2 and rebless authority must be assigned in this bead.

## Files / Scope

- `sentinels/tools/<name>.json` (tool manifest + ground-truth AX label)
- `sentinels/scenarios/<name>.yaml`
- `sentinels/expected.json` (expected bucket assignments)
- `Makefile` — `sentinel-check` target
- `.github/workflows/sentinel-gate.yml`
- `docs/sentinels/rebless-procedure.md` (named approver + escalation path)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] At least 6 sentinel tools covering: good baseline, ambiguous-name, missing-required, redundant-with-sibling, oversized-description, undocumented-coupling
- [ ] `make sentinel-check` runs the harness against all sentinels and asserts bucket assignments match `sentinels/expected.json`
- [ ] Variance across **N=5 re-runs** is < 0.5 per metric (R17 acceptance)
- [ ] Sentinel failures **block release** via the `sentinel-gate.yml` GitHub Action
- [ ] `docs/sentinels/rebless-procedure.md` names a single approver (not "the team") and a documented unblock SLA
- [ ] At least 2 maintainers (bus-factor-2) listed as code owners on `sentinels/`

## Dependencies

- bead 04 (schemas) — sentinels emit typed artifacts
acceptance_criteria: ≥6 sentinels; make sentinel-check passes; variance <0.5/metric across N=5; sentinel-gate.yml blocks release; named rebless approver; bus-factor-2 code owners.

title: Schemas + AX#### rule registry + mcp-ax explain
description:
## Goal

Implement the typed-artifact schema layer + AX#### rule registry + `mcp-ax explain <ID>` verb. This is the foundation every other CLI stage depends on.

## Why

R1 (typed artifacts), R3 (stable AX#### IDs + explain), R6-lite (claims schema), R11 (provenance + averaging-across-sources ban). The premortem flags free-text fields as the averaging-circumvention vector — Theme D mitigation requires no free-text in the default surface.

## Files / Scope

- `harness/schemas/findings.json`
- `harness/schemas/claims.json` (R6-lite: `{assertion, evidence_pointer, claim_class}`; `claim_class` enum **open registry** keyed `AX####-style`, NOT 4-value enum — Theme D mitigation)
- `harness/schemas/trace.jsonl.schema.json` (includes required `mode` field — R26)
- `harness/schemas/metrics.json`
- `harness/schemas/run.json` (R13 reproducibility metadata)
- `harness/schemas/report.json` (regression_delta required top-level field — R2)
- `harness/rules/<AXxxxx>.yaml` (per-rule sheet: `{title, rationale, citation, auto_fix, severity_default, evidence_template, one_line_summary, rule_version_hash}`)
- `harness/rules/_index.yaml` (every assigned code)
- `cli/explain.{ts,py}` — `mcp-ax explain AX0007` prints the rule sheet
- `tools/lint-schemas.{ts,py}` — CI check: ID uniqueness, no orphan rules, no free-text in default surface

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All schemas under `harness/schemas/` validate sample fixtures via `jsonschema` or equivalent in CI
- [ ] `mcp-ax explain AX0001` (or any registered ID) prints the full rule sheet from `harness/rules/AX0001.yaml`
- [ ] Schema CI fails when two rule files share an ID
- [ ] Schema CI fails when a rule referenced in `findings.json` is missing from the registry
- [ ] Schema CI fails when any default-surface schema introduces a free-text field (no `notes`, no `description` outside the rule registry)
- [ ] `claims.json` schema does NOT include `score`, `weight`, `threshold`, or `coverage` — all aggregation lives in `--scorecard` adapter (Tension-1 default-output rule)
- [ ] `claim_class` is an open registry (e.g. `claim_class: AX-CC-fix-correct`), not a hard enum — addresses Theme D
- [ ] `report.json` schema rejects documents missing `regression_delta` (R2 acceptance)
- [ ] `trace.jsonl.schema.json` requires `mode` field on every record (R26 acceptance)
- [ ] `run.json` includes: orchestrator/test/judge model capability-profile (NOT model_id — P1 mitigation), prompt-template SHAs, harness git SHA, tool manifest hash, MCP server endpoint + version + build SHA, tested-repo commit SHA, RNG seed, timestamps, total cost, replay vs live flag

## Dependencies

None (foundation layer).
acceptance_criteria: All harness/schemas/*.json validate; mcp-ax explain prints rule sheet; CI rejects ID dupes/orphan rules/free-text in default surface; claim_class is open AX-CC-* registry; report.json requires regression_delta; trace records require mode field; run.json uses capability-profile not model_id.

title: Decompose mcp-ax-ph3 epic via /research-project → PRD → /prd-build
description:
Epic mcp-ax-ph3 ('MCP tool agentic experience evaluation: build evaluation harness, add scoring dimensions, create test suites for common MCP patterns') has no children. Per sjarmak direction 2026-04-29: best path is research-project skill → PRD → prd-build skill for parallel work-unit decomposition.

## Step 1 — research-project

Run `/research-project` skill on this question:
"What's the best architecture for an MCP tool agentic-experience evaluation harness? Coverage: scoring dimensions (correctness, latency, error-recovery, tool-call efficiency, verbosity), test-suite shape for common MCP patterns (resource fetch, tool execution, prompt-based, stdio vs http), CI integration, and reporting. Aim: a harness that ranks MCP tools on agentic-friendliness empirically."

Output: docs/prd/mcp_evaluation_harness.md (risk-annotated PRD per the skill spec).

## Step 2 — prd-build

Run `/prd-build` skill on the PRD from step 1. Decomposes into parallel work units, files them as child beads of mcp-ax-ph3.

## Step 3

Mail mayor: 'mcp-ax-ph3 decomposed into N child beads. PRD at <path>. Ready for routing.'

## Acceptance

- PRD at docs/prd/mcp_evaluation_harness.md, with risks + premortem
- mcp-ax-ph3 has 5-15 child beads (each a parallelizable work unit)
- Each child bead has clear AC + files-to-touch
- Mail to mayor flagging completion

title: Finalize workflow
close_reason: orphaned formula scaffolding — wisp beads from stale mol-focus-review run. See dr-1zac9.

title: Commit, summarize, and hand off to Codex reviewer
description:
Your implementation is done and tests pass. Now hand off to a separate
Codex agent for /simplify and /code-review.

**1. Ensure everything is committed:**
```bash
git status
```
If uncommitted changes, commit them:
```bash
git add <files>
git commit -m "<type>: <description>"
```

**2. Record what you did on the bead:**
```bash
bd update mcp-ax-ph3.1 --notes "Implementation complete. Summary: <what was done, key decisions, files changed>. Ready for review."
```

**3. Record the diff summary for the reviewer:**
```bash
DIFF_STAT=$(git diff --stat main...HEAD)
bd update mcp-ax-ph3.1 --set-metadata impl_complete=true
bd update mcp-ax-ph3.1 --set-metadata base_branch=main
bd update mcp-ax-ph3.1 --set-metadata diff_stat="$DIFF_STAT"
```

**4. Hand off to the Codex review agent:**
```bash
gc sling /home/ds/scix_experiments/codex-reviewer mcp-ax-ph3.1 --no-formula
```

This routes the bead to the Codex reviewer. The reviewer will run
/simplify and /code-review, then either close the bead (pass) or
reject it back to you with a rejection reason.

**5. Drain — you are done:**
```bash
gc runtime drain-ack
```

**Exit criteria:** Bead handed off to reviewer, implementation agent drained.
close_reason: orphaned formula scaffolding — wisp beads from stale mol-focus-review run. See dr-1zac9.

title: Run tests to verify implementation
description:
**Config: test_command = **

Run the project test suite to verify your implementation:

```bash

```

If test_command is empty, skip this step.

If tests fail:
1. Read the failure output carefully
2. Fix the issue in your implementation
3. Commit the fix
4. Re-run tests until they pass

Do NOT proceed until tests pass.

**Exit criteria:** All tests pass (or no test command configured).
close_reason: orphaned formula scaffolding — wisp beads from stale mol-focus-review run. See dr-1zac9.

title: Execute /focus on the bead
description:
Invoke the /focus skill with the assigned bead:

/focus mcp-ax-ph3.1

The /focus skill will:
1. Read the bead and plan the implementation
2. Execute the plan step by step
3. Verify the work against acceptance criteria

Follow the skill's instructions completely. Do not override its workflow.
If the skill asks for decisions, make them based on the bead context.

**If context filling up:** Save progress, commit work, then:
```bash
gc runtime request-restart
```

**Exit criteria:** /focus has completed its execution cycle.
close_reason: Skill /research-project executed per bead description (override of generic /focus step)

title: Load context and understand assignment
description:
Initialize your session and understand the work.

```bash
gc prime
bd prime
bd show mcp-ax-ph3.1
gc mail inbox
```

Read the bead's title, description, acceptance criteria, design notes,
and any linked context. Understand what "done" looks like before starting.

If the bead has `metadata.rejection_reason`, a prior attempt failed.
Read the rejection reason and focus on fixing the specific issue.

**Exit criteria:** You understand the work and acceptance criteria.
close_reason: Context loaded: research on MCP AX evaluation topic, invoke /research-project with 3 perspectives

title: mol-focus-review
description:
Focus + external review formula — Claude implements via /focus, then a separate
Codex agent runs /simplify and /code-review.

Two-agent workflow:
1. Claude agent: /focus on bead (plan → execute → verify) → run tests → commit
2. Handoff: Claude slings the bead to a Codex review agent
3. Codex agent: /simplify → /code-review → close or reject

The implementation and review are done by DIFFERENT agents to ensure
independent quality assessment. Codex is used for review because it provides
a different perspective from the implementing Claude agent.

## Variables

| Variable | Source | Description |
|----------|--------|-------------|
| issue    | caller | The work bead ID to focus on |
| base_branch | caller | Base branch for diff comparison (default: main) |
| test_command | rig config | Test command to verify work. Empty = skip. |
| review_agent | caller | Codex review agent path (default: codex-reviewer) |

title: mol-focus-review
description:
Focus + in-session review formula — one Claude worker implements via /focus,
then reviews its own work with /simplify and /code-review before closing.

Single-agent workflow:
1. Load context and understand the bead (including prior rejection_reason).
2. /focus on bead (plan → execute → verify).
3. Run tests.
4. /simplify the diff.
5. /code-review the diff and verify the work matches the bead's acceptance
   criteria. If review finds blockers, set `metadata.rejection_reason` and
   drain — a fresh pool worker picks up the bead and retries, reading the
   rejection reason in step 1.
6. If review passes, commit final state, close the bead, drain.

Review in step 5 is a hard verification gate: it must check that the
implementation actually fulfills what the bead asked for, not just that
the code is stylistically clean. Missing acceptance criteria → reject.

## Variables

| Variable | Source | Description |
|----------|--------|-------------|
| issue    | caller | The work bead ID to focus on |
| base_branch | caller | Base branch for diff comparison (default: main) |
| test_command | rig config | Test command to verify work. Empty = skip. |

title: mcp-ax trace --record live recording (deferred from ph3.9)
description:
## Goal

Implement the live-recording path for `mcp-ax trace --record`. Currently stubbed in harness/runtime/cassette.py with LiveRecordingNotConfiguredError pending the proxy bead (mcp-ax-ph3.13) which lands MCP server credentials and the LLM client.

## Why

ph3.9 deferred this so the deterministic cassette-replay path (load-bearing core per premortem) could ship first. Live recording is required to refresh cassettes and to onboard new MCP servers.

## Files / Scope

- harness/runtime/cassette.py — replace live_record_not_configured() with a real recorder
- harness/runtime/live_agent.py (new) — LLM + MCP client glue
- cli/trace.py — wire --record / --no-cassette flags through to live recorder
- Tests: tests/test_record_live.py (mocked LLM + MCP server fixtures)

## Dependencies

- mcp-ax-ph3.9 (closed) — provides the cassette engine + replay path
- mcp-ax-ph3.13 — provides MCP proxy/credentials infrastructure
acceptance_criteria: live --record path connects to MCP server + LLM client; captures JSONL cassette for each scenario; --no-cassette runs live without writing; ANTHROPIC_API_KEY + MCP_SERVER_ENDPOINT credentials wired.
notes: Live recording path delivered. harness/runtime/live_agent.py owns the recorder loop (ZFC: LLM does reasoning, recorder owns structure + R8 self-score check + cassette shape). harness/runtime/anthropic_client.py adapts the Anthropic SDK; harness/runtime/mcp_session.py wraps the async mcp SDK behind a sync MCPClient via a background event-loop thread (supports http/sse/stdio transports). harness/runtime/live_factory.py reads ANTHROPIC_API_KEY + MCP_SERVER_ENDPOINT and is the monkeypatch seam for tests. cli/trace.py wires --record (writes cassettes + traces) and --no-cassette (traces only, no cassette); --record and --no-cassette together is now a usage error rather than silently contradictory. harness/runtime/cassette.write_cassette serialises records into canonical JSONL and enforces the 500 KB per-scenario budget at write time. tests/test_record_live.py: 15 tests covering recorder happy path, schema-invalid args, empty-result detection, max-turns abort, tool-failure capture, cassette round-trip + oversize rejection, CLI env-var requirements, mutual-exclusion of flags, in-process live happy path with monkeypatched factories, and budget cap. All 160 in-repo tests + sentinel-check + schema-lint green.
